---
title: "LRP102: Mechanism model — taught receptive vocabulary (TR) -> letter sounds (L), concurrent"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP102 estimates the adjusted **concurrent** association between taught receptive
vocabulary ($TR$) and letter-sound knowledge ($L$; DAG symbol $LS$) across *all three
phase transitions* (t1$\to$t2, t2$\to$t3, t3$\to$t4) with phase-specific intercepts.
The mechanism and the outcome are read at the **same wave**
($TR_{\text{post}} \to L_{\text{post}}$), conditioning on the child's own
letter-sound baseline $L_{\text{pre}}$. It turns the descriptive finding that taught vocabulary is a strong
*concurrent* correlate of letter sounds (pooled $r \approx 0.62$, rising across waves)
into a confounder-adjusted slope (#405, companion to #404).

::: {.callout-important title="Exploratory edge-check — not a hypothesised causal path"}
The revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`) posits **no vocabulary $\to$
letter-sound edge**: $TR \to \{TE, RV, EV, LF, RG, WR\}$ and none of those reach $LS$,
so there is no directed $TR \to LS$ path. Letter sounds' parents are cognitive ability,
hearing, speech and the intervention — not vocabulary — which is why the letter-sound
gain model `gf-004` carries no skill adjusters. Any association estimated here is
therefore **entirely confounding / selection**, dominated by the latent
general-ability ($GA$) common cause that cannot be fully closed. Read it as a
latent-ability-confounded adjusted association, an edge-check on the committed DAG,
**never** a mechanism.
:::

_Linear mechanism (estimand note)._ The mechanism enters as a **linear slope**
$\beta_{\text{mech}}$ on $\text{logit}_*(TR_{\text{post}}, N_{TR})$, not the 1-D HSGP
curve used elsewhere in this family, following the vocabulary-exposure precedent
(LRP56/57, `mech-088`): the nonparametric curve does not converge on vocabulary
predictors at reporting tier, and DAG-required adjusters are not dropped to buy
convergence. The estimand is the **linear** $TR \to L$ adjusted association — a single
slope, not a shape.

_Concurrent vs lagged._ This is the **concurrent** readout. Its lagged / predictive
sibling **LRP103** takes $TR$ at period-start
($TR_{\text{pre}} \to L_{\text{post}} \mid L_{\text{pre}}$) and is the design that
actually tests *prediction of letter-sound growth*. A positive concurrent slope here alongside a null lagged slope in LRP103 is
the expected "tracks, but doesn't drive" signature.

## Adjustment set

Re-derived for the $TR$--$LS$ backdoor under the revised DAG (2026-07-10). Parents of
$TR$: $\{A, GA, HS, IG, IS, RW\}$; parents of $LS$: $\{A, GA, HS, IG, IS, SP\}$. The
model conditions on the observable common causes:

- $G$ — group; always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on linearly (a $\gamma_A\,z(\text{age})$ term).
- $HS$ — hearing status (`hs` / `hs_missing`); common cause $TR \leftarrow HS \to LS$.
- $IS$ — intervention sessions (`attend`); common cause $TR \leftarrow IS \to LS$ (the
  intervention teaches both vocabulary and letter sounds). Adjusted, with the
  $IG \to IS \leftarrow GA$ collider closed at the always-conditioned arm $G$ (the
  d-separation argument of `mech-088`).
- $SP$ — speech production (`deapp_c` / `deapp_c_missing`); **not** a parent of $TR$,
  so not a confounder of this slope, but a strong, stable, pre-treatment parent of
  $LS$, kept as a **precision adjuster**. Every
  $TR \leftarrow \{A, GA, HS\} \to SP \to LS$ backdoor is already blocked at $A$ /
  $HS$ / the $GA$ proxy, so conditioning on it cannot bias the slope. It also keeps
  the $TR$ and $TE$ (LRP104) adjustment sets
  identical, where $SP$ *is* a required confounder.
- $L_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline letter sounds (autoregressive term; temporal precedence,
  not a collider).

_Deliberately **not** adjusted:_

- $RW$ — phonological memory (`erbto`). Unlike `mech-088` ($TR \to WR$),
  $RW \to \{TE, EV, TR, RV, PA, NW, PS\}$ **never reaches $LS$**, so it is not a
  common cause of $TR$ and $LS$. Dropping it is the key re-derivation for the
  letter-sound outcome.
- $TE$, $RV$ — descendants of $TR$; conditioning would block legitimate indirect paths.
- $GA$ — latent and unadjustable; the child random intercept proxies its time-invariant
  part, so the slope stays an adjusted association.

### Interpretation caveats

- *No unmeasured confounder assumption* for $(TR_{\text{post}}, L_{\text{post}})$:
  strong and not testable, and the latent $GA$ is a named violation direction that
  cannot be closed. The DAG posits no causal $TR \to LS$ edge, so a non-zero slope is expected
  to reflect that residual common-cause structure.
- $TR_{\text{post}}$ is measured with error; non-negligible error attenuates the slope.
- Taught-vocabulary variation is largely intervention-generated, so the slope describes
  covariation within a treated system.
- Across-phase pooling assumes a stable structural relationship over time.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
